name: Lithuanian Strawberry Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together 1 1/4 cups of the sugar and the butter until very light and fluffy.
2. Add egg yolks, one at a time, beating well after each; continue beating until very fluffy, about 5 minutes.
3. Sift together flour, baking powder, and 1/2 teaspoon salt; stir into creamed mixture.
4. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form; gradually add remaining 1/4 cup sugar, beating to stiff peaks.
5. Fold the beaten egg whites into the creamed mixture.
6. Pour batter into 3 lightly greased and floured 9 x 1 1/2-inch round baking pans.
7. Bake at 350°F until done, about 20 minutes.
8. Cool for 10 minutes; remove from pans. Cool on a rack.
9. Whip cream and vanilla just to soft peaks.
10. Place a cake layer on a serving plate. Spread with currant jelly and then with 1 cup of the whipped cream. Sprinkle with 2 tablespoons pecans.
11. Spread second layer with 1/3 cup of the strawberry preserves, then with 1 cup of the whipped cream; sprinkle with 2 tablespoons pecans.
12. Gently place atop the first layer.
13. Carefully top with the third layer.
14. Frost the top and sides with the remaining whipped cream; coat the sides with the remaining pecans.
15. Using a small spoon, dot the top with the remaining strawberry preserves.
